translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moab rebelled against Israel after the death of Ahab.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ahaziah fell down through the lattice in his upper room that was in Samaria, and was sick. So he sent messengers, and said to them, “Go, inquire of Baal Zebub, the god of Ekron, whether I will recover of this sickness.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Yahweh’s  angel said to Elijah the Tishbite, “Arise, go up to meet the messengers of the king of Samaria, and tell them, ‘Is it because there is no God  in Israel, that you go to inquire of Baal Zebub, the god of Ekron?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore Yahweh says, “You will not come down from the bed where you have gone up, but you will surely die.”’” Then Elijah departed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The messengers returned to him, and he said to them, “Why is it that you have returned?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said to him, “A man came up to meet us, and said to us, ‘Go, return to the king who sent you, and tell him, “Yahweh says, ‘Is it because there is no God in Israel, that you send to inquire of Baal Zebub, the god of Ekron? Therefore you will not come down from the bed where you have gone up, but you will surely die.’”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “What kind of man was he who came up to meet you, and told you these words?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They answered him, “He was a hairy man, and wearing a leather belt around his waist.” He said, “It’s Elijah the Tishbite.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the king sent a captain of fifty with his fifty to him. He went up to him; and behold,  he was sitting on the top of the hill. He said to him, “Man of God, the king has said, ‘Come down!’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elijah answered to the captain of fifty, “If I am a man of God, then let fire come down from the sky, and consume you and your fifty!” Then fire came down from the sky, and consumed him and his fifty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again he sent to him another captain of fifty with his fifty. He answered him, “Man of God, the king has said, ‘Come down quickly!’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elijah answered them, “If I am a man of God, then let fire come down from the sky, and consume you and your fifty!” Then God’s fire came down from the sky, and consumed him and his fifty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again he sent the captain of a third fifty with his fifty. The third captain of fifty went up, and came and fell on his knees before Elijah, and begged him, and said to him, “Man of God, please let my life, and the life of these fifty of your servants, be precious in your sight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, fire came down from the sky, and consumed the last two captains of fifty with their fifties. But now let my life be precious in your sight.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s angel said to Elijah, “Go down with him. Don’t be afraid of him.” Then he arose, and went down with him to the king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him, “Yahweh says, ‘Because you have sent messengers to inquire of Baal Zebub, the god of Ekron, is it because there is no God in Israel to inquire of his word? Therefore you will not come down from the bed where you have gone up, but you will surely die.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So he died according to Yahweh’s word which Elijah had spoken. Jehoram began to reign in his place in the second year of Jehoram the son of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, because he had no son.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Ahaziah which he did, aren’t they written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Yahweh was about to take Elijah up by a whirlwind into heaven, Elijah went with Elisha from Gilgal.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elijah said to Elisha, “Please wait here, for Yahweh has sent me as far as Bethel.” Elisha said, “As Yahweh lives, and as your soul lives, I will not leave you.” So they went down to Bethel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of the prophets who were at Bethel came out to Elisha, and said to him, “Do you know that Yahweh will take away your master from your head today?” He said, “Yes, I know it. Hold your peace.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elijah said to him, “Elisha, please wait here, for Yahweh has sent me to Jericho.” He said, “As Yahweh lives, and as your soul lives, I will not leave you.” So they came to Jericho.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of the prophets who were at Jericho came near to Elisha, and said to him, “Do you know that Yahweh will take away your master from your head today?” He answered, “Yes, I know it. Hold your peace.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elijah said to him, “Please wait here, for Yahweh has sent me to the Jordan.” He said, “As Yahweh lives, and as your soul lives, I will not leave you.” Then they both went on.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Fifty men of the sons of the prophets went, and stood opposite them at a distance; and they both stood by the Jordan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elijah took his mantle, and rolled it up, and struck the waters, and they were divided here and there, so that they both went over on dry ground.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had gone over, Elijah said to Elisha, “Ask what I shall do for you, before I am taken from you.” Elisha said, “Please let a double portion of your spirit be on me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “You have asked a hard thing. If you see me when I am taken from you, it will be so for you; but if not, it will not be so.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they continued on and talked, behold, a chariot of fire and horses of fire separated them, and Elijah went up by a whirlwind into heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha saw it, and he cried, “My father, my father, the chariots of Israel and its horsemen!” He saw him no more. Then he took hold of his own clothes, and tore them in two pieces.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also took up Elijah’s mantle that fell from him, and went back, and stood by the bank of the Jordan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took Elijah’s mantle that fell from him, and struck the waters, and said, “Where is Yahweh, the God of Elijah?” When he also had struck the waters, they were divided apart, and Elisha went over.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the sons of the prophets who were at Jericho over against him saw him, they said, “The spirit of Elijah rests on Elisha.” They came to meet him, and bowed themselves to the ground before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said to him, “See now, there are with your servants fifty strong men. Please let them go and seek your master. Perhaps Yahweh’s Spirit has taken him up, and put him on some mountain, or into some valley. He said, “Don’t send them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they urged him until he was ashamed, he said, “Send them.” Therefore they sent fifty men; and they searched for three days, but didn’t find him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came back to him, while he stayed at Jericho; and he said to them, “Didn’t I tell you, ‘Don’t go?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The men of the city said to Elisha, “Behold, please, the situation of this city is pleasant, as my lord sees; but the water is bad, and the land is barren.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Bring me a new jar, and put salt in it.” Then they brought it to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went out to the spring of the waters, and threw salt into it, and said, “Yahweh says, ‘I have healed these waters. There shall not be from there any more death or barren wasteland.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the waters were healed to this day, according to Elisha’s word which he spoke.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went up from there to Bethel. As he was going up by the way, some youths came out of the city and mocked him, and said to him, “Go up, you baldy! Go up, you baldy!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He looked behind him and saw them, and cursed them in Yahweh’s name. Then two female bears came out of the woods, and mauled forty-two of those youths.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went from there to Mount Carmel, and from there he returned to Samaria.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Jehoram the son of Ahab began to reign over Israel in Samaria in the eighteenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, and reigned twelve years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight, but not like his father, and like his mother, for he put away the pillar of Baal that his father had made.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless he held to the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, with which he made Israel to sin. He didn’t depart from them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Mesha king of Moab was a sheep breeder; and he supplied the king of Israel with the wool of one hundred thousand lambs, and of one hundred thousand rams.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when Ahab was dead, the king of Moab rebelled against the king of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King Jehoram went out of Samaria at that time, and mustered all Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went and sent to Jehoshaphat the king of Judah, saying, “The king of Moab has rebelled against me. Will you go with me against Moab to battle?” He said, “I will go up. I am as you are, my people as your people, my horses as your horses.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Which way shall we go up?” He answered, “The way of the wilderness of Edom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the king of Israel went with the king of Judah and the king of Edom, and they marched for seven days along a circuitous route. There was no water for the army or for the animals that followed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Israel said, “Alas! For Yahweh has called these three kings together to deliver them into the hand of Moab.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jehoshaphat said, “Isn’t there a prophet of Yahweh here, that we may inquire of Yahweh by him?” One of the king of Israel’s servants answered, “Elisha the son of Shaphat, who poured water on the hands of Elijah, is here.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat said, “Yahweh’s word is with him.” So the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat and the king of Edom went down to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha said to the king of Israel, “What have I to do with you? Go to the prophets of your father, and to the prophets of your mother.” The king of Israel said to him, “No, for Yahweh has called these three kings together to deliver them into the hand of Moab.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha said, “As Yahweh of Armies lives, before whom I stand, surely, were it not that I respect the presence of Jehoshaphat the king of Judah, I would not look toward you, nor see you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But now bring me a musician.” When the musician played, Yahweh’s hand came on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Yahweh says, ‘Make this valley full of trenches.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh says, ‘You will not see wind, neither will you see rain, yet that valley will be filled with water, and you will drink, both you and your livestock and your other animals.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is an easy thing in Yahweh’s sight. He will also deliver the Moabites into your hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall strike every fortified city, and every choice city, and shall fell every good tree, and stop all springs of water, and mar every good piece of land with stones.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the morning, about the time of offering the sacrifice, behold, water came by the way of Edom, and the country was filled with water.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when all the Moabites heard that the kings had come up to fight against them, they gathered themselves together, all who were able to put on armor, young and old, and stood on the border.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They rose up early in the morning, and the sun shone on the water, and the Moabites saw the water opposite them as red as blood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said, “This is blood. The kings are surely destroyed, and they have struck each other. Now therefore, Moab, to the plunder!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they came to the camp of Israel, the Israelites rose up and struck the Moabites, so that they fled before them; and they went forward into the land attacking the Moabites.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They beat down the cities; and on every good piece of land each man cast his stone, and filled it. They also stopped all the springs of water, and felled all the good trees, until in Kir Hareseth all they left was its stones; however the men armed with slings went around it, and attacked it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the king of Moab saw that the battle was too severe for him, he took with him seven hundred men who drew a sword, to break through to the king of Edom; but they could not.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he took his oldest son who would have reigned in his place, and offered him for a burnt offering on the wall. There was great wrath against Israel; and they departed from him, and returned to their own land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now a certain woman of the wives of the sons of the prophets cried out to Elisha, saying, “Your servant my husband is dead. You know that your servant feared Yahweh. Now the creditor has come to take for himself my two children to be slaves.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha said to her, “What should I do for you? Tell me: what do you have in the house?” She said, “Your servant has nothing in the house, except a pot of oil.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he said, “Go, borrow empty containers from of all your neighbors. Don’t borrow just a few containers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Go in and shut the door on you and on your sons, and pour oil into all those containers; and set aside those which are full.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So she went from him, and shut the door on herself and on her sons. They brought the containers to her, and she poured oil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the containers were full, she said to her son, “Bring me another container.” He said to her, “There isn’t another container.” Then the oil stopped flowing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then she came and told the man of God. He said, “Go, sell the oil, and pay your debt; and you and your sons live on the rest.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One day Elisha went to Shunem, where there was a prominent woman; and she persuaded him to eat bread. So it was, that as often as he passed by, he turned in there to eat bread.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She said to her husband, “See now, I perceive that this is a holy man of God who passes by us continually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Please let us make a little room on the roof. Let us set for him there a bed, a table, a chair, and a lamp stand. When he comes to us, he can stay there.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One day he came there, and he went to the room and lay there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to Gehazi his servant, “Call this Shunammite.” When he had called her, she stood before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him, “Say now to her, ‘Behold, you have cared for us with all this care. What is to be done for you? Would you like to be spoken for to the king, or to the captain of the army?’” She answered, “I dwell among my own people.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “What then is to be done for her?” Gehazi answered, “Most certainly she has no son, and her husband is old.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Call her.” When he had called her, she stood in the door.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “At this season, when the time comes around, you will embrace a son.” She said, “No, my lord, you man of God, do not lie to your servant.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The woman conceived, and bore a son at that season, when the time came around, as Elisha had said to her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the child was grown, one day he went out to his father to the reapers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to his father, “My head! My head!” He said to his servant, “Carry him to his mother.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had taken him, and brought him to his mother, he sat on her knees until noon, and then died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She went up and laid him on the man of God’s bed, and shut the door on him, and went out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She called to her husband, and said, “Please send me one of the servants, and one of the donkeys, that I may run to the man of God, and come again.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Why would you want go to him today? It is not a new moon or a Sabbath.” She said, “It’s alright.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then she saddled a donkey, and said to her servant, “Drive, and go forward! Don’t slow down for me, unless I ask you to.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So she went, and came to the man of God to Mount Carmel. When the man of God saw her afar off, he said to Gehazi his servant, “Behold, there is the Shunammite.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Please run now to meet her, and ask her, ‘Is it well with you? Is it well with your husband? Is it well with your child?’” She answered, “It is well.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When she came to the man of God to the hill, she caught hold of his feet. Gehazi came near to thrust her away; but the man of God said, “Leave her alone; for her soul is troubled within her; and Yahweh has hidden it from me, and has not told me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then she said, “Did I ask you for a son, my lord? Didn’t I say, ‘Do not deceive me’?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he said to Gehazi, “Tuck your cloak into your belt, take my staff in your hand, and go your way. If you meet any man, don’t greet him; and if anyone greets you, don’t answer him again. Then lay my staff on the child’s face.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The child’s mother said, “As Yahweh lives, and as your soul lives, I will not leave you.” So he arose, and followed her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Gehazi went ahead of them, and laid the staff on the child’s face; but there was no voice and no hearing. Therefore he returned to meet him, and told him, “The child has not awakened.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Elisha had come into the house, behold, the child was dead, and lying on his bed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went in therefore, and shut the door on them both, and prayed to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went up, and lay on the child, and put his mouth on his mouth, and his eyes on his eyes, and his hands on his hands. He stretched himself on him; and the child’s flesh grew warm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he returned, and walked in the house once back and forth; and went up, and stretched himself out on him. Then the child sneezed seven times, and the child opened his eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called Gehazi, and said, “Call this Shunammite!” So he called her. When she had come in to him, he said, “Take up your son.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then she went in, fell at his feet, and bowed herself to the ground; then she picked up her son, and went out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha came again to Gilgal. There was a famine in the land; and the sons of the prophets were sitting before him; and he said to his servant, “Get the large pot, and boil stew for the sons of the prophets.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One went out into the field to gather herbs, and found a wild vine, and gathered a lap full of wild gourds from it, and came and cut them up into the pot of stew; for they didn’t recognize them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they poured out for the men to eat. As they were eating some of the stew, they cried out, and said, “Man of God, there is death in the pot!” And they could not eat it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he said, “Then bring meal.” He threw it into the pot; and he said, “Serve it to the people, that they may eat.” And there was nothing harmful in the pot.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man from Baal Shalishah came, and brought the man of God bread some of the first fruits: twenty loaves of barley, and fresh ears of grain in his sack. He said, “Give to the people, that they may eat.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His servant said, “What, should I set this before a hundred men?” But he said, “Give the people, that they may eat; for Yahweh says, ‘They will eat, and will have some left over.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So he set it before them, and they ate, and had some left over, according to Yahweh’s word.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Naaman, captain of the army of the king of Syria, was a great man with his master, and honorable, because by him Yahweh had given victory to Syria: he was also a mighty man of valor, but he was a leper.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Syrians had gone out in bands, and had brought away captive out of the land of Israel a little maiden; and she waited on Naaman’s wife.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She said to her mistress, “I wish that my lord were with the prophet who is in Samaria! Then he would heal him of his leprosy.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Someone went in, and told his lord, saying, “The maiden who is from the land of Israel said this.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Syria said, “Go now, and I will send a letter to the king of Israel.” He departed, and took with him ten talents  of silver, and six thousand pieces of gold, and ten changes of clothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought the letter to the king of Israel, saying, “Now when this letter has come to you, behold, I have sent Naaman my servant to you, that you may heal him of his leprosy.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the king of Israel had read the letter, he tore his clothes, and said, “Am I God, to kill and to make alive, that this man sends to me to heal a man of his leprosy? But please consider and see how he seeks a quarrel against me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It was so, when Elisha the man of God heard that the king of Israel had torn his clothes, that he sent to the king, saying, “Why have you torn your clothes? Let him come now to me, and he shall know that there is a prophet in Israel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Naaman came with his horses and with his chariots, and stood at the door of the house of Elisha.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha sent a messenger to him, saying, “Go and wash in the Jordan seven times, and your flesh shall come again to you, and you shall be clean.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Naaman was angry, and went away, and said, “Behold, I thought, ‘He will surely come out to me, and stand, and call on the name of Yahweh his God, and wave his hand over the place, and heal the leper.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Aren’t Abanah and Pharpar, the rivers of Damascus, better than all the waters of Israel? Couldn’t I wash in them, and be clean?” So he turned and went away in a rage.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His servants came near, and spoke to him, and said, “My father, if the prophet had asked you do some great thing, wouldn’t you have done it? How much rather then, when he says to you, ‘Wash, and be clean?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then went he down, and dipped himself seven times in the Jordan, according to the saying of the man of God; and his flesh was restored like the flesh of a little child, and he was clean.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He returned to the man of God, he and all his company, and came, and stood before him; and he said, “See now, I know that there is no God in all the earth, but in Israel. Now therefore, please take a gift from your servant.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he said, “As Yahweh lives, before whom I stand, I will receive none.” He urged him to take it; but he refused.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Naaman said, “If not, then, please let two mules’ burden of earth be given to your servant; for your servant will from now on offer neither burnt offering nor sacrifice to other gods, but to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In this thing may Yahweh pardon your servant: when my master goes into the house of Rimmon to worship there, and he leans on my hand, and I bow myself in the house of Rimmon. When I bow myself in the house of Rimmon, may Yahweh pardon your servant in this thing.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him, “Go in peace.” So he departed from him a little way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Gehazi the servant of Elisha the man of God, said, “Behold, my master has spared this Naaman the Syrian, in not receiving at his hands that which he brought. As Yahweh lives, I will run after him, and take something from him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Gehazi followed after Naaman. When Naaman saw one running after him, he came down from the chariot to meet him, and said, “Is all well?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “All is well. My master has sent me, saying, ‘Behold, even now two young men of the sons of the prophets have come to me from the hill country of Ephraim. Please give them a talent  of silver and two changes of clothing.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Naaman said, “Be pleased to take two talents.” He urged him, and bound two talents of silver in two bags, with two changes of clothing, and laid them on two of his servants; and they carried them before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he came to the hill, he took them from their hand, and stored them in the house. Then he let the men go, and they departed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he went in, and stood before his master. Elisha said to him, “Where did you come from, Gehazi?” He said, “Your servant went nowhere.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him, “Didn’t my heart go with you, when the man turned from his chariot to meet you? Is it a time to receive money, and to receive garments, and olive groves and vineyards, and sheep and cattle, and male servants and female servants?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore the leprosy of Naaman will cling to you and to your offspring  forever.” He went out from his presence a leper, as white as snow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of the prophets said to Elisha, “See now, the place where we live and meet with you is too small for us.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Please let us go to the Jordan, and each man take a beam from there, and let us make us a place there, where we may live.” He answered, “Go!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One said, “Please be pleased to go with your servants.” He answered, “I will go.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So he went with them. When they came to the Jordan, they cut down wood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as one was cutting down a tree, the ax head fell into the water. Then he cried, and said, “Alas, my master! For it was borrowed.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The man of God asked, “Where did it fall?” He showed him the place. He cut down a stick, threw it in there, and made the iron float.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Take it.” So he put out his hand and took it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the king of Syria was at war against Israel; and he took counsel with his servants, saying, “My camp will be in such and such a place.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The man of God sent to the king of Israel, saying, “Beware that you not pass this place; for the Syrians are coming down there.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Israel sent to the place which the man of God told him and warned him of; and he saved himself there, not once or twice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Syria’s heart was very troubled about this. He called his servants, and said to them, “Won’t you show me which of us is for the king of Israel?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One of his servants said, “No, my lord, O king; but Elisha, the prophet who is in Israel, tells the king of Israel the words that you speak in your bedroom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Go and see where he is, that I may send and get him.” He was told, “Behold, he is in Dothan.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore he sent horses, chariots, and a great army there. They came by night, and surrounded the city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the servant of the man of God had risen early, and gone out, behold, an army with horses and chariots was around the city. His servant said to him, “Alas, my master! What shall we do?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered, “Don’t be afraid; for those who are with us are more than those who are with them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha prayed, and said, “Yahweh, please open his eyes, that he may see.” Yahweh opened the young man’s eyes; and he saw: and behold, the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire around Elisha.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they came down to him, Elisha prayed to Yahweh, and said, “Please strike this people with blindness.” He struck them with blindness according to Elishah’s word.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha said to them, “This is not the way, neither is this the city. Follow me, and I will bring you to the man whom you seek.” He led them to Samaria.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had come into Samaria, Elisha said, “Yahweh, open these men’s eyes, that they may see.” Yahweh opened their eyes, and they saw; and behold, they were in the middle of Samaria.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Israel said to Elisha, when he saw them, “My father, shall I strike them? Shall I strike them?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered, “You shall not strike them. Would you strike those whom you have taken captive with your sword and with your bow? Set bread and water before them, that they may eat and drink, and go to their master.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He prepared great feast for them. When they had eaten and drunk, he sent them away, and they went to their master. So the bands of Syria stopped raiding the land of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this, Benhadad king of Syria gathered all his army, and went up and besieged Samaria.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was a great famine in Samaria. Behold, they besieged it, until a donkey’s head was sold for eighty pieces of silver, and the fourth part of a kab  of dove’s dung for five pieces of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As the king of Israel was passing by on the wall, a woman cried to him, saying, “Help, my lord, O king!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “If Yahweh doesn’t help you, where could I get help for you? From of the threshing floor, or from the wine press?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said to her, “What is your problem?” She answered, “This woman said to me, ‘Give your son, that we may eat him today, and we will eat my son tomorrow.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So we boiled my son, and ate him: and I said to her on the next day, ‘Give your son, that we may eat him;’ and she has hidden her son.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the king heard the words of the woman, he tore his clothes. Now he was passing by on the wall, and the people looked, and behold, he had sackcloth underneath on his body.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he said, “God do so to me, and more also, if the head of Elisha the son of Shaphat stays on him today.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Elisha was sitting in his house, and the elders were sitting with him. Then the king sent a man from before him; but before the messenger came to him, he said to the elders, “Do you see how this son of a murderer has sent to take away my head? Behold, when the messenger comes, shut the door, and hold the door shut against him. Isn’t the sound of his master’s feet behind him?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While he was still talking with them, behold, the messenger came down to him. Then he said, “Behold, this evil is from Yahweh. Why should I wait for Yahweh any longer?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha said, “Hear Yahweh’s word. Yahweh says, ‘Tomorrow about this time a seah  of fine flour will be sold for a shekel,  and two seahs of barley for a shekel, in the gate of Samaria.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the captain on whose hand the king leaned answered the man of God, and said, “Behold, if Yahweh made windows in heaven, could this thing be?” He said, “Behold, you will see it with your eyes, but will not eat of it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now there were four leprous men at the entrance of the gate. They said to one another, “Why do we sit here until we die?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If we say, ‘We will enter into the city,’ then the famine is in the city, and we will die there. If we sit still here, we also die. Now therefore come, and let us surrender to the army of the Syrians. If they save us alive, we will live; and if they kill us, we will only die.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They rose up in the twilight, to go to the camp of the Syrians. When they had come to the outermost part of the camp of the Syrians, behold, no man was there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Lord  had made the army of the Syrians to hear the sound of chariots, and the sound of horses, even the noise of a great army; and they said to one another, “Behold, the king of Israel has hired against us the kings of the Hittites and the kings of the Egyptians to attack us.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore they arose and fled in the twilight, and left their tents, and their horses, and their donkeys, even the camp as it was, and fled for their life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When these lepers came to the outermost part of the camp, they went into one tent, and ate and drink, and carried away silver, gold, and clothing, and went and hid it. Then they came back, and entered into another tent, and carried things from there also, and went and hid them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then they said to one another, “We aren’t doing right. Today is a day of good news, and we keep silent. If we wait until the morning light, punishment will overtake us. Now therefore come, let’s go and tell the king’s household.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they came and called to the city gatekeepers; and they told them, “We came to the camp of the Syrians, and, behold, there was no man there, not even a man’s voice, but the horses tied, and the donkeys tied, and the tents as they were.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called the gatekeepers; and they told it to the king’s household within.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king arose in the night, and said to his servants, “I will now show you what the Syrians have done to us. They know that we are hungry. Therefore are they gone out of the camp to hide themselves in the field, saying, ‘When they come out of the city, we shall take them alive, and get into the city.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One of his servants answered, “Please let some people take five of the horses that remain, which are left in the city. Behold, they are like all the multitude of Israel who are left in it. Behold, they are like all the multitude of Israel who are consumed. Let us send and see.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore they took two chariots with horses; and the king them sent out to the Syrian army, saying, “Go and see.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went after them to the Jordan; and behold, all the path was full of garments and equipment which the Syrians had cast away in their haste. The messengers returned, and told the king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The people went out and plundered the camp of the Syrians. So a seah  of fine flour was sold for a shekel, and two measures of barley for a shekel,  according to Yahweh’s word.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king appointed the captain on whose hand he leaned to be in charge of the gate; and the people trampled over him in the gate, and he died as the man of God had said, who spoke when the king came down to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It happened as the man of God had spoken to the king, saying, “Two seahs  of barley for a shekel,  and a seah of fine flour for a shekel, shall be tomorrow about this time in the gate of Samaria”;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and that captain answered the man of God, and said, “Now, behold, if Yahweh made windows in heaven, might such a thing be?” and he said, “Behold, you will see it with your eyes, but will not eat of it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It happened like that to him; for the people trampled over him in the gate, and he died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Elisha had spoken to the woman whose son he had restored to life, saying, “Arise, and go, you and your household, and stay for a while wherever you can; for Yahweh has called for a famine. It will also come on the land for seven years.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The woman arose, and did according to the man of God’s word. She went with her household, and lived in the land of the Philistines for seven years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the end of seven years, the woman returned from the land of the Philistines. Then she went out to beg the king for her house and for her land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the king was talking with Gehazi the servant of the man of God, saying, “Please tell me all the great things that Elisha has done.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he was telling the king how he had restored to life him who was dead, behold, the woman, whose son he had restored to life, begged the king for her house and for her land. Gehazi said, “My lord, O king, this is the woman, and this is her son, whom Elisha restored to life.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the king asked the woman, she told him. So the king appointed to her a certain officer, saying, “Restore all that was hers, and all the fruits of the field since the day that she left the land, even until now.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha came to Damascus; and Benhadad the king of Syria was sick. He was told, “The man of God has come here.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said to Hazael, “Take a present in your hand, and go, meet the man of God, and inquire of Yahweh by him, saying, ‘Will I recover from this sickness?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Hazael went to meet him, and took a present with him, even of every good thing of Damascus, forty camels’ burden, and came and stood before him, and said, “Your son Benhadad king of Syria has sent me to you, saying, ‘Will I recover from this sickness?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha said to him, “Go, tell him, ‘You will surely recover;’ however Yahweh has shown me that he will surely die.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He settled his gaze steadfastly on him, until he was ashamed. Then the man of God wept.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hazael said, “Why do you weep, my lord?” He answered, “Because I know the evil that you will do to the children of Israel. You will set their strongholds on fire, and you will kill their young men with the sword, and will dash their little ones in pieces, and rip up their pregnant women.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hazael said, “But what is your servant, who is but a dog, that he could do this great thing?” Elisha answered, “Yahweh has shown me that you will be king over Syria.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he departed from Elisha, and came to his master, who said to him, “What did Elisha say to you?” He answered, “He told me that you would surely recover.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the next day, he took a thick cloth, dipped it in water, and spread it on his face, so that he died. Then Hazael reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the fifth year of Joram the son of Ahab king of Israel, Jehoshaphat being king of Judah then, Jehoram the son of Jehoshaphat king of Judah began to reign.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was thirty-two years old when he began to reign. He reigned eight years in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He walked in the way of the kings of Israel, as did Ahab’s house; for he married Ahab’s daughter. He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However Yahweh would not destroy Judah, for David his servant’s sake, as he promised him to give to him a lamp for his children always.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In his days Edom revolted from under the hand of Judah, and made a king over themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Joram passed over to Zair, and all his chariots with him: and he rose up by night, and struck the Edomites who surrounded him, and the captains of the chariots; and the people fled to their tents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Edom revolted from under the hand of Judah to this day. Then Libnah revolted at the same time.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rest of the acts of Joram, and all that he did, aren’t they written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joram slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in David’s city; and Ahaziah his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the twelfth year of Joram the son of Ahab king of Israel, Ahaziah the son of Jehoram king of Judah began to reign.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ahaziah was twenty-two years old when he began to reign; and he reigned one year in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Athaliah the daughter of Omri king of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He walked in the way of Ahab’s house, and did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight, as did Ahab’s house; for he was the son-in-law of Ahab’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went with Joram the son of Ahab to war against Hazael king of Syria at Ramoth Gilead, and the Syrians wounded Joram.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King Joram returned to be healed in Jezreel from the wounds which the Syrians had given him at Ramah, when he fought against Hazael king of Syria. Ahaziah the son of Jehoram king of Judah went down to see Joram the son of Ahab in Jezreel, because he was sick.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha the prophet called one of the sons of the prophets, and said to him, “Put your belt on your waist, take this vial of oil in your hand, and go to Ramoth Gilead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When you come there, find Jehu the son of Jehoshaphat the son of Nimshi, and go in, and make him rise up from among his brothers, and take him to an inner room.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then take the vial of oil, and pour it on his head, and say, ‘Yahweh says, “I have anointed you king over Israel.”’ Then open the door, flee, and don’t wait.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the young man, even the young man, the prophet, went to Ramoth Gilead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he came, behold, the captains of the army were sitting. Then he said, “I have a message for you, captain.” Jehu said, “To which of us all?” He said, “To you, O captain.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He arose, and went into the house. Then he poured the oil on his head, and said to him, “Yahweh, the God of Israel, says, ‘I have anointed you king over the people of Yahweh, even over Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You must strike your master Ahab’s house, that I may avenge the blood of my servants the prophets, and the blood of all the servants of Yahweh, at the hand of Jezebel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the whole house of Ahab will perish. I will cut off from Ahab everyone who urinates against a wall,  both him who is shut up and him who is left at large in Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will make Ahab’s house like the house of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha the son of Ahijah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The dogs will eat Jezebel on the plot of ground of Jezreel, and there shall be no one to bury her.’” Then he opened the door and fled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jehu came out to the servants of his lord, and one said to him, “Is all well? Why did this mad fellow come to you?” He said to them, “You know the man and how he talks.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said, “That is a lie. Tell us now.” He said, “He said to me, ‘Yahweh says, I have anointed you king over Israel.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then they hurried, and each man took his cloak, and put it under him on the top of the stairs, and blew the trumpet, saying, “Jehu is king.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Jehu the son of Jehoshaphat the son of Nimshi conspired against Joram. (Now Joram was keeping Ramoth Gilead, he and all Israel, because of Hazael king of Syria;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but king Joram had returned to be healed in Jezreel of the wounds which the Syrians had given him, when he fought with Hazael king of Syria.) Jehu said, “If this is your thinking, then let no one escape and go out of the city, to go to tell it in Jezreel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Jehu rode in a chariot and went to Jezreel, for Joram lay there. Ahaziah king of Judah had come down to see Joram.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the watchman was standing on the tower in Jezreel, and he spied the company of Jehu as he came, and said, “I see a company.” Joram said, “Take a horseman, and send to meet them, and let him say, ‘Is it peace?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So one went on horseback to meet him, and said, “Thus says the king, ‘Is it peace?’” Jehu said, “What do you have to do with peace? Fall in behind me!” The watchman said, “The messenger came to them, but he isn’t coming back.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he sent out a second on horseback, who came to them, and said, “Thus says the king, ‘Is it peace?’” Jehu answered, “What do you have to do with peace? Fall in behind me!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The watchman said, “He came to them, and isn’t coming back. The driving is like the driving of Jehu the son of Nimshi, for he drives furiously.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joram said, “Get ready!” They got his chariot ready. Then Joram king of Israel and Ahaziah king of Judah went out, each in his chariot, and they went out to meet Jehu, and found him on Naboth the Jezreelite’s land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Joram saw Jehu, he said, “Is it peace, Jehu?” He answered, “What peace, so long as the prostitution of your mother Jezebel and her witchcraft abound?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joram turned his hands, and fled, and said to Ahaziah, “This is treason, Ahaziah!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehu drew his bow with his full strength, and struck Joram between his arms; and the arrow went out at his heart, and he sunk down in his chariot.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Jehu said to Bidkar his captain, “Pick him up, and throw him in the plot of the field of Naboth the Jezreelite; for remember how, when you and I rode together after Ahab his father, Yahweh laid this burden on him:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘Surely I have seen yesterday the blood of Naboth, and the blood of his sons,’ says Yahweh; ‘and I will repay you in this plot of ground,’ says Yahweh. Now therefore take and cast him onto the plot of ground, according to Yahweh’s word.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when Ahaziah the king of Judah saw this, he fled by the way of the garden house. Jehu followed after him, and said, “Strike him also in the chariot!” They struck him at the ascent of Gur, which is by Ibleam. He fled to Megiddo, and died there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His servants carried him in a chariot to Jerusalem, and buried him in his tomb with his fathers in David’s city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the eleventh year of Joram the son of Ahab, Ahaziah began to reign over Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jehu had come to Jezreel, Jezebel heard of it; and she painted her eyes, and adorned her head, and looked out at the window.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As Jehu entered in at the gate, she said, “Do you come in peace, Zimri, you murderer of your master?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He lifted up his face to the window, and said, “Who is on my side? Who?” Two or three eunuchs looked out at him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Throw her down!” So they threw her down; and some of her blood was sprinkled on the wall, and on the horses. Then he trampled her under foot.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had come in, he ate and drank. Then he said, “See now to this cursed woman, and bury her; for she is a king’s daughter.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went to bury her, but they found no more of her than the skull, the feet, and the palms of her hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore they came back, and told him. He said, “This is Yahweh’s word, which he spoke by his servant Elijah the Tishbite, saying, ‘The dogs will eat the flesh of Jezebel on the plot of Jezreel,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the body of Jezebel will be as dung on the face of the field on Jezreel’s land, so that they won’t say, “This is Jezebel.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Ahab had seventy sons in Samaria. Jehu wrote letters, and sent to Samaria, to the rulers of Jezreel, even the elders, and to those who brought up Ahab’s sons, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now as soon as this letter comes to you, since your master’s sons are with you, and you have chariots and horses, a fortified city also, and armor,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Select the best and fittest of your master’s sons, set him on his father’s throne, and fight for your master’s house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But they were exceedingly afraid, and said, “Behold, the two kings didn’t stand before him! How then shall we stand?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who was over the household, and he who was over the city, the elders also, and those who raised the children, sent to Jehu, saying, “We are your servants, and will do all that you ask us. We will not make any man king. You do that which is good in your eyes.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he wrote a letter the second time to them, saying, “If you are on my side, and if you will listen to my voice, take the heads of the men who are your master’s sons, and come to me to Jezreel by tomorrow this time.” Now the king’s sons, being seventy persons, were with the great men of the city, who brought them up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the letter came to them, they took the king’s sons and killed them, even seventy people, and put their heads in baskets, and sent them to him to Jezreel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A messenger came and told him, “They have brought the heads of the king’s sons.” He said, “Lay them in two heaps at the entrance of the gate until the morning.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the morning, he went out, and stood, and said to all the people, “You are righteous. Behold, I conspired against my master and killed him, but who killed all these?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Know now that nothing will fall to the earth of Yahweh’s word, which Yahweh spoke concerning Ahab’s house. For Yahweh has done that which he spoke by his servant Elijah.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Jehu struck all that remained of Ahab’s house in Jezreel, with all his great men, his familiar friends, and his priests, until he left him no one remaining.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He arose and departed, and went to Samaria. As he was at the shearing house of the shepherds on the way,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehu met with the brothers of Ahaziah king of Judah, and said, “Who are you?” They answered, “We are the brothers of Ahaziah. We are going down to greet the children of the king and the children of the queen.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Take them alive!” They took them alive, and killed them at the pit of the shearing house, even forty-two men. He didn’t leave any of them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had departed from there, he met Jehonadab the son of Rechab coming to meet him. He greeted him, and said to him, “Is your heart right, as my heart is with your heart?” Jehonadab answered, “It is.” “If it is, give me your hand.” He gave him his hand; and he took him up to him into the chariot.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Come with me, and see my zeal for Yahweh.” So they made him ride in his chariot.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he came to Samaria, he struck all who remained to Ahab in Samaria, until he had destroyed him, according to Yahweh’s word, which he spoke to Elijah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehu gathered all the people together, and said to them, “Ahab served Baal a little; but Jehu will serve him much.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore call to me all the prophets of Baal, all of his worshipers, and all of his priests. Let no one be absent; for I have a great sacrifice to Baal. Whoever is absent, he shall not live.” But Jehu did deceptively, intending to destroy the worshipers of Baal.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehu said, “Sanctify a solemn assembly for Baal!” So they proclaimed it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehu sent through all Israel; and all the worshipers of Baal came, so that there was not a man left that didn’t come. They came into the house of Baal; and the house of Baal was filled from one end to another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him who kept the wardrobe, “Bring out robes for all the worshipers of Baal!” So he brought robes out to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehu went with Jehonadab the son of Rechab into the house of Baal. Then he said to the worshipers of Baal, “Search, and see that none of the servants of Yahweh are here with you, but only the worshipers of Baal.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they went in to offer sacrifices and burnt offerings. Now Jehu had appointed for himself eighty men outside, and said, “If any of the men whom I bring into your hands escape, he who lets him go, his life shall be for the life of him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As soon as he had finished offering the burnt offering, Jehu said to the guard and to the captains, “Go in and kill them! Let no one escape.” So they struck them with the edge of the sword. The guard and the captains threw the bodies out, and went to the inner shrine of the house of Baal.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought out the pillars that were in the house of Baal, and burned them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They broke down the pillar of Baal, and broke down the house of Baal, and made it a latrine, to this day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus Jehu destroyed Baal out of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However, Jehu didn’t depart from the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, with which he made Israel to sin, the golden calves that were in Bethel and that were in Dan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said to Jehu, “Because you have done well in executing that which is right in my eyes, and have done to Ahab’s house according to all that was in my heart, your descendants shall sit on the throne of Israel to the fourth generation.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jehu took no heed to walk in the law of Yahweh, the God of Israel, with all his heart. He didn’t depart from the sins of Jeroboam, with which he made Israel to sin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In those days Yahweh began to cut away parts of Israel; and Hazael struck them in all the borders of Israel;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: from the Jordan eastward, all the land of Gilead, the Gadites, and the Reubenites, and the Manassites, from Aroer, which is by the valley of the Arnon, even Gilead and Bashan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Jehu, and all that he did, and all his might, aren’t they written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehu slept with his fathers; and they buried him in Samaria. Jehoahaz his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The time that Jehu reigned over Israel in Samaria was twenty-eight years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when Athaliah the mother of Ahaziah saw that her son was dead, she arose and destroyed all the royal offspring.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jehosheba, the daughter of king Joram, sister of Ahaziah, took Joash the son of Ahaziah, and stole him away from among the king’s sons who were slain, even him and his nurse, and put them in the bedroom; and they hid him from Athaliah, so that he was not slain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was with her hidden in Yahweh’s house six years while Athaliah reigned over the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the seventh year Jehoiada sent and fetched the captains over hundreds of the Carites and of the guard, and brought them to him into Yahweh’s house; and he made a covenant with them, and made a covenant with them in Yahweh’s house, and showed them the king’s son.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He commanded them, saying, “This is what you must do: a third of you, who come in on the Sabbath, shall be keepers of the watch of the king’s house;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: a third of you shall be at the gate Sur; and a third of you at the gate behind the guard. So you shall keep the watch of the house, and be a barrier.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The two companies of you, even all who go out on the Sabbath, shall keep the watch of Yahweh’s house around the king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You shall surround the king, every man with his weapons in his hand; and he who comes within the ranks, let him be slain. Be with the king when he goes out, and when he comes in.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The captains over hundreds did according to all that Jehoiada the priest commanded; and they each took his men, those who were to come in on the Sabbath, with those who were to go out on the Sabbath, and came to Jehoiada the priest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The priest delivered to the captains over hundreds the spears and shields that had been king David’s, which were in Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The guard stood, every man with his weapons in his hand, from the right side of the house to the left side of the house, along by the altar and the house, around the king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he brought out the king’s son, and put the crown on him, and gave him the covenant; and they made him king, and anointed him; and they clapped their hands, and said, “Long live the king!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Athaliah heard the noise of the guard and of the people, she came to the people into Yahweh’s house:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and she looked, and behold, the king stood by the pillar, as the tradition was, with the captains and the trumpets by the king; and all the people of the land rejoiced, and blew trumpets. Then Athaliah tore her clothes, and cried, “Treason! Treason!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoiada the priest commanded the captains of hundreds who were set over the army, and said to them, “Bring her out between the ranks. Kill anyone who follows her with the sword.” For the priest said, “Don’t let her be slain in Yahweh’s house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they made way for her; and she went by the way of the horses’ entry to the king’s house, and she was slain there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoiada made a covenant between Yahweh and the king and the people, that they should be Yahweh’s people; also between the king and the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people of the land went to the house of Baal, and broke it down; his altars and his images broke they in pieces thoroughly, and killed Mattan the priest of Baal before the altars. The priest appointed officers over Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took the captains over hundreds, and the Carites, and the guard, and all the people of the land; and they brought down the king from Yahweh’s house, and came by the way of the gate of the guard to the king’s house. He sat on the throne of the kings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So all the people of the land rejoiced, and the city was quiet. They had slain Athaliah with the sword at the king’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoash was seven years old when he began to reign.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoash began to reign in the seventh year of Jehu, and he reigned forty years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Zibiah of Beersheba.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoash did that which was right in Yahweh’s eyes all his days in which Jehoiada the priest instructed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However the high places were not taken away. The people still sacrificed and burned incense in the high places.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoash said to the priests, “All the money of the holy things that is brought into Yahweh’s house, in current money, the money of the people for whom each man is evaluated,  and all the money that it comes into any man’s heart to bring into Yahweh’s house,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: let the priests take it to them, each man from his donor; and they shall repair the damage to the house, wherever any damage is found.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But it was so, that in the twenty-third year of king Jehoash the priests had not repaired the damage to the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then king Jehoash called for Jehoiada the priest, and for the other priests, and said to them, “Why don’t you repair the damage to the house? Now therefore take no more money from your treasurers, but deliver it for repair of the damage to the house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The priests consented that they should take no more money from the people, and not repair the damage to the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jehoiada the priest took a chest, and bored a hole in its lid, and set it beside the altar, on the right side as one comes into Yahweh’s house; and the priests who kept the threshold put all the money that was brought into Yahweh’s house into it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they saw that there was much money in the chest, the king’s scribe and the high priest came up, and they put it in bags and counted the money that was found in Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They gave the money that was weighed out into the hands of those who did the work, who had the oversight of Yahweh’s house; and they paid it out to the carpenters and the builders, who worked on Yahweh’s house,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and to the masons and the stone cutters, and for buying timber and cut stone to repair the damage to Yahweh’s house, and for all that was laid out for the house to repair it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But there were not made for Yahweh’s house cups of silver, snuffers, basins, trumpets, any vessels of gold, or vessels of silver, of the money that was brought into Yahweh’s house;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for they gave that to those who did the work, and repaired Yahweh’s house with it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover they didn’t demand an accounting from the men into whose hand they delivered the money to give to those who did the work; for they dealt faithfully.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The money for the trespass offerings, and the money for the sin offerings was not brought into Yahweh’s house. It was the priests’.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Hazael king of Syria went up, and fought against Gath, and took it; and Hazael set his face to go up to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoash king of Judah took all the holy things that Jehoshaphat and Jehoram and Ahaziah, his fathers, kings of Judah, had dedicated, and his own holy things, and all the gold that was found in the treasures of Yahweh’s house, and of the king’s house, and sent it to Hazael king of Syria; and he went away from Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Joash, and all that he did, aren’t they written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His servants arose, and made a conspiracy, and struck Joash at the house of Millo, on the way that goes down to Silla.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Jozacar the son of Shimeath, and Jehozabad the son of Shomer, his servants, struck him, and he died; and they buried him with his fathers in David’s city; and Amaziah his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the twenty-third year of Joash the son of Ahaziah, king of Judah, Jehoahaz the son of Jehu began to reign over Israel in Samaria for seventeen years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight, and followed the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, with which he made Israel to sin. He didn’t depart from it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s anger burned against Israel, and he delivered them into the hand of Hazael king of Syria, and into the hand of Benhadad the son of Hazael, continually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoahaz begged Yahweh, and Yahweh listened to him; for he saw the oppression of Israel, how the king of Syria oppressed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (Yahweh gave Israel a savior, so that they went out from under the hand of the Syrians; and the children of Israel lived in their tents as before.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless they didn’t depart from the sins of the house of Jeroboam, with which he made Israel to sin, but walked in them; and the Asherah also remained in Samaria.)

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he didn’t leave to Jehoahaz of the people any more than fifty horsemen, and ten chariots, and ten thousand footmen; for the king of Syria destroyed them, and made them like the dust in threshing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoahaz, and all that he did, and his might, aren’t they written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoahaz slept with his fathers; and they buried him in Samaria; and Joash his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the thirty-seventh year of Joash king of Judah, Jehoash the son of Jehoahaz began to reign over Israel in Samaria for sixteen years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight. He didn’t depart from all the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, with which he made Israel to sin; but he walked in them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Joash, and all that he did, and his might with which he fought against Amaziah king of Judah, aren’t they written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joash slept with his fathers; and Jeroboam sat on his throne. Joash was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Elisha became sick with the illness of which he died; and Joash the king of Israel came down to him, and wept over him, and said, “My father, my father, the chariots of Israel and its horsemen!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha said to him, “Take bow and arrows”; and he took bow and arrows for himself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the king of Israel, “Put your hand on the bow”; and he put his hand on it. Elisha laid his hands on the king’s hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Open the window eastward”; and he opened it. Then Elisha said, “Shoot!” and he shot. He said, “Yahweh’s arrow of victory, even the arrow of victory over Syria; for you will strike the Syrians in Aphek, until you have consumed them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Take the arrows”; and he took them. He said to the king of Israel, “Strike the ground”; and he struck three times, and stopped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The man of God was angry with him, and said, “You should have struck five or six times. Then you would have struck Syria until you had consumed it; whereas now you will strike Syria just three times.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elisha died, and they buried him. Now the bands of the Moabites invaded the land at the coming in of the year.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they were burying a man, behold, they saw a band of raiders; and they threw the man into Elisha’s tomb. As soon as the man touched Elisha’s bones, he revived, and stood up on his feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hazael king of Syria oppressed Israel all the days of Jehoahaz.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Yahweh was gracious to them, and had compassion on them, and had respect for them, because of his covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and would not destroy them, and he didn’t cast them from his presence as yet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hazael king of Syria died; and Benhadad his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoash the son of Jehoahaz took again out of the hand of Benhadad the son of Hazael the cities which he had taken out of the hand of Jehoahaz his father by war. Joash struck him three times, and recovered the cities of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the second year of Joash son of Joahaz king of Israel Amaziah the son of Joash king of Judah began to reign.

translations.name: World English Bible
